controlnet.notes: Standard humanoid tuning for 7 bipeds. Wolf-Rider uses same settings — treat as single combined subject, not separable rider+mount.

subjects.risk_notes: Mounted unit breaks body-plan uniformity. Must ship as single combined subject. If 48px readability fails, cut variant — do not redesign pack around it.

production_notes: 7 variants are humanoid bipeds, 1 (wolf-rider) is mounted — treat wolf-rider as risk lane
Each variant MUST have a distinct silhouette at 48px — review contact sheets side by side
Anti-convergence law: posture + head shape + gear silhouette + threat read + body mass must all differ per variant
Pack-wide palette should feel coherent: green skin tones (olive to forest to sage), earth-tone gear, dark metals
Direction readability must hold across all 8 views for every variant
Reject any generation that collapses into 'same goblin, different prop' — check body_lock in each config
Key confusion pairs to watch: Scout vs Grunt (crouch+hood vs standing+bare), Brute vs Warchief (bare+wide vs armored+tall)
Wolf-rider ships as combined unit. If readability at 48px fails after 3 generation attempts, cut it and ship 7-variant pack
